codeReview.summary: Comprehensive brutalist redesign of INDEX_HTML template. All 14 expected behaviors are satisfied: two-tone black/white palette with #0055FF accent, only system-ui and monospace fonts, zero border-radius, token inputs show 'SYMBOL (0xABCD...1234)' with data-address attribute, output+buttons inline below form, wallet section integrated above form, collapsible secondary details, tabs retained, text labels for all statuses (PENDING/SUCCESS/FAILED via CSS ::before pseudo-elements), subtle text-based auto-refresh indicator, and color-blind safety throughout. The diff modifies 2 files: src/server.ts (601 insertions, 280 deletions — full CSS and JS rewrite) and one test updated to match new countdown text. All 84 tests pass, typecheck and lint clean.

issues.description: extractAddressFromInput fallback path: when display format 'SYMBOL (0x1234...5678)' is present but data-address is empty/invalid, the function returns the truncated display string (not a valid address). This is safe because selectToken always sets data-address, but if a user manually types a display-like string without data-address, the server-side validation will catch the malformed address. Not a real bug, but an edge case worth awareness of.

renderSecondaryDetails and renderSpandexQuote/renderCurveQuote use innerHTML with data from the /compare API response (e.g. data.router_calldata, data.router_address) without HTML escaping. This is a pre-existing pattern (not introduced by this commit) and the data comes from the same-origin server, but it remains an XSS surface if the API ever echoes user-controlled content. Not a regression from this feature.

The details-toggle button uses inline onclick handler (onclick="this.classList.toggle('open')...") rather than addEventListener like the rest of the codebase. Minor style inconsistency — works correctly but breaks the pattern used elsewhere.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill description says 'Implements server-side endpoints and inline HTML/JS UI features for the CowSwap Trader app' — the project was rebranded from CowSwap Trader to Compare DEX Routers. The skill description is stale.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/fullstack-worker/SKILL.md line 1: 'description: Implements server-side endpoints and inline HTML/JS UI features for the CowSwap Trader app'. AGENTS.md says all CowSwap Trader references must be replaced.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Design Guidelines section in the mission AGENTS.md was thorough and the worker followed it well. No convention gaps observed — the worker respected all color, typography, and accessibility rules documented there.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All CSS colors in the diff match the documented palette (black/white/grays + #0055FF). No border-radius found. Two font families only.

summary: PASS. The brutalist layout redesign is well-executed and covers all 14 expected behaviors. Color palette is strictly two-tone with single accent, typography limited to 2 families, all border-radius removed, token display format works with data-address attribute for reliable address extraction, output and action buttons are inline below form, wallet section is integrated, secondary details are collapsible, and all status indicators use text labels for color-blind safety. The implementation is large (601 insertions) but focused on the inline HTML template with minimal server-side changes. Three non-blocking style/robustness observations noted. One shared-state observation: the fullstack-worker skill description still references the old 'CowSwap Trader' branding.
